output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here’s How to Fight Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and send a demand letter in 60 seconds for only $19.99. No signup needed.
content_outline: Why Landlords Illegally Withhold Security Deposits
What Your Landlord Can (and Cannot) Deduct From Your Deposit
State Deadlines: How Long Is Too Long?
How to Write a Security Deposit Demand Letter That Actually Works
Get Your Money Back Without a Lawyer Using Deposits-Back.com
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days.
Normal wear and tear cannot be legally deducted from your deposit.
A formal demand letter is the fastest, most effective first step to recover your money.
Deposits-Back.com generates a state-specific demand letter in under 60 seconds for a one-time $19.99 fee—no sign-up and no subscription.
output.cta: Don’t let your landlord keep what’s yours. Generate your deposit return demand letter now at Deposits-Back.com for just $19.99—no sign-up, no subscription, and it takes less than 60 seconds.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlord is withholding, deducting, or refusing to return their security deposit.

social_hooks.hook_text: POV: You moved out 3 weeks ago and your landlord is 'keeping' your $2,000 deposit for 'cleaning' 🚩
social_hooks.script: You left the place spotless. Now they’re ghosting you. Before you panic, know this: in most states, they have 14 to 30 days to give it back. If they don’t, a demand letter puts the law on your side. Deposits-Back dot com. 60 seconds. 19 bucks. No signup. Get your money.

social_hooks.keyword_cluster: what can landlord deduct from security deposit
social_hooks.hook_text: Your landlord charged you $400 for a 'paint touch-up'… but is that even legal? 🤔
social_hooks.script: Normal wear and tear? They can’t charge you for that. Scuffed floors, faded paint, small nail holes—that’s on them. If they’re deducting bogus fees, fight back with a demand letter. Deposits-Back dot com. 19 dollars. One minute. Done.

social_hooks.hook_text: They said they’re keeping your deposit because you broke the lease. But can they actually do that? 📄
social_hooks.script: Maybe—but only for actual damages or unpaid rent, not as a punishment. If they’re bluffing, call it out. A demand letter forces them to prove every deduction or pay up. Go to Deposits-Back dot com. Generate your letter in under a minute for 19 bucks.
